LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF OWES THE TAXES PER SECTION 902.1(A). BECAUSE THE LANGUAGE OF THE ORDINANCE IS CLEAR AND UNEQUIVOCAL, STATEMENTS MADE BY CITY OFFICIALS CONTRARY TO THE LANGUAGE OF SECTION 902.1(A) ARE NOT ENTITLED TO ANY WEIGHT AND DO NOT SUPPORT AN ESTOPPEL ARGUMENT. THE ESTOPPEL ARGUMENT ALSO FAILS BECAUSE THE PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PRESENT ANY EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT SUCH AN ARGUMENT. THE TAX, AS APPLIED TO THE PLAINTIFF, IS NOT AN IMPERMISSIBLE INCOME TAX. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-29-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
Mtn To Tax Memo Of Costs
Off Calendar Jul-30-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. THOMAS HU, TEL. NO. (650) 621-0009 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. STEPHANIE PROFITT, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4690 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF HERMAN HU D.D.S., A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION's motion To Tax Memo Of Costs IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION BY JUDGE CURTIS KARNOW. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: The motion to tax costs is denied. The costs memorandum was timely filed (CCP 1013). Exempt fees are nonetheless recoverable. The courier fees appear reasonably necessary. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Curtis Karnow, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =(302/CK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-03-2012 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-06-2012 Set For Case Management Conference On Jul-18-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEPHANIE PROFITT, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4660 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Granted. Plaintiff owes the taxes per section 902.1(a). Because the language of the ordinance is clear and unequivocal, statements made by city officials contrary to the language of section 902.1(a) are not entitled to any weight and do not support an estoppel argument. The estoppel argument also fails because the plaintiff does not present any evidence to support such an argument. The tax, as applied to the plaintiff, is not an impermissible income tax. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Not reported. =(302/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEPHANIE PROFITT, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4660 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF HERMAN HU D.D.S., A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION'S CROSS- MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Off calendar as moot in light of the ruling on the City's motion for summary judgment. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Not reported. =(302/HEK)
